blocks.content: Free Gene (4)

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: How did she detect me?
Vedium, though Lycan's father, was no longer a biological being. His body was composed of electricity, and since he could control the movement of electric charges, he could go anywhere in the world.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: Is it an angel's ability?
While remaining in Amy's room in the form of free electrons, he had overheard every conversation. Through this, he learned that Ikael was an angel, but her exact abilities were still unknown to him.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: No ordinary human—or even an extraordinary one—could track my movements in this state. Therefore…
A direct confrontation would be a losing battle.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Answer me.
As Ikael took a step forward, her face was illuminated by the moonlight streaming through the window.
The eyes on Vedium's white mask changed into heart shapes, but the atmosphere remained icy.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: That girl… is Shirone's lover, isn't she?

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Shirone?

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: Yes. Shirone is my target. If I kill her, I can destabilize Shirone emotionally.
Vedium was honest because he needed to buy time and find a way to escape.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: No matter how quickly I react, I won't be able to escape her senses.
If her abilities rivaled those of a scale mage who could manipulate space, her mobility would be formidable.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Hmm…
Thinking she had cornered her prey, Ikael rested her chin on her hand, lost in thought.
Vedium remained frozen, unable to move, but he knew she wasn't mistaken in her assessment.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Why… do you want to kill Shirone?

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: That brat…
The simple white mask, which usually only displayed basic emotions, now transformed with intricate, complex lines, forming a vivid depiction of a demonic face.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: …killed my son.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: What?
At that moment, Vedium's body disintegrated back into free electrons.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Damn it!
By the time Ikael regained her focus, the flow of electric charges had become too faint to trace.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: He planned this.
He only needed to hold Ikael's attention for the brief moment when electricity lingered in space.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: What a formidable human. But if Shirone really killed his son, what happened?
Ikael figured she could ask Shirone about it in the morning, but she quickly revised her thoughts.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Shirone is my son.
In any situation, protecting her child came first.
Watching over the still-sleeping Amy, Ikael's body once again dissolved into radiant light.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: I'll handle this myself.
A silent explosion of light filled the room, leaving behind an eerie calm, as if nothing had happened.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: What a strange situation.
In his free-electron state, Vedium roamed the air, sensing Ikael's presence.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: While he could read human brainwaves, he couldn't fully decipher their thoughts, especially in terms of language.
Angels, however, were different. Through their radiant forms, he could directly sense their thoughts.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: Ikael abruptly changed direction, ignoring inertia.
She's heading there.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: Vedium had managed to fool her senses while inside Amy's room.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: Amplification?
He had only been able to evade her because her senses hadn't been heightened at the time.
Now, however, Ikael's perception had become so keen that she could hear even the faint breathing of a sleeping butterfly in the forest.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: He's gone.
With a faint popping sound, Ikael came to a halt.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: He's erased his thoughts.
In his free-electron state, Vedium's presence was virtually zero.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: I don't know how you came to have such a body…

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: The cessation of thought is akin to the cessation of existence. You won't be able to remain in that state forever.
A breeze swept through the silence.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Come out. I want to hear your story. Depending on what you say, I may yield.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: His son was dead.
Though she now had Shirone, Ikael understood the pain of losing a child.
Crackle!
Sparks erupted, igniting the grass ten meters away from Ikael.
Amidst swirling ash, Vedium materialized once more, his body taking human form.
The mask was blank, devoid of expression.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: An angel, huh? Interesting.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: You're a strong human, too. You said Shirone killed your son. Is that true?

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: Yes.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: If Shirone was at fault, then perhaps—

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: No need for that.
Vedium raised a hand, cutting her off.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: My son was also an assassin. He probably tried to kill Shirone first and got killed in return. It's just business. I have no personal grudge against Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: 'Kill your emotions to achieve what your emotions desire,' is that it?

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: …You're not very angelic. I thought angels would be colder and more rational.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: No being is truly devoid of emotions.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: So, why did you track me? Do you intend to eliminate any potential threats to Shirone in advance?

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: I'm a parent, too. Even if there's a valid reason, grief doesn't simply vanish. If you're willing to accept an apology, can you forgive Shirone?

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: No.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: Vedium's response was firm.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: I've already been paid, and there's more money to collect after I complete the job. Not fully grasping your abilities was my mistake, but I won't make the same mistake twice.
The symbols in his eyes changed to dollar signs ($).

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: If it's money you want, I can give it to you. Name your price, and I'll get it.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: No.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Why?

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: I can't let him down…

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: Lycan.
For Ikael, those words cut the deepest.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Fine.
Her delicate fist was now aimed at Vedium.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: If it's a matter of emotion, then I'll act on mine. One strike. If I can subdue you with a single blow, you'll let this go. If not, you may do as you wish.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: You'll regret this.
His body began to glow with a bluish light.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: I'll show you my best technique. I guarantee it's unlike anything you've seen before.
Even if it meant eternal annihilation.
Without a word, Ikael remained still, her fist poised.
Vedium dissolved into electricity.
At the same moment, blue sparks scattered in every direction.
Driven solely by a desire to kill, Vedium charged toward Ikael with precision akin to nature's most primal instincts.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Now!
Even as time slowed to a crawl and Vedium's attack drew near, Ikael didn't flinch.
Instead, a radiant magical field unfolded.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Ataraxia.
Ignoring the relativity of time, her sensitivity heightened to the extreme, allowing her to sense even the faintest movements of electrons.
She twisted her body with blinding speed.
Thud!
Her outstretched fist twisted midair, striking Vedium's mask with tremendous force.
Crack.
The mask fractured, and Vedium's body was hurled backward like an electric cannon.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: Arghhh!
His white mask, once floating alone above the plains, was now pulled down by gravity.
An expression with drooping eyes appeared on its surface.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: That was close.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: I didn't think you'd break your promise. But I'd like to know—what happened to your body?

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: Grrk… creak…
For a moment, the mask emitted mechanical noises before Vedium's voice returned.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: Why does it matter? Life's full of things no one wants to hear. Your son's alive, isn't he? That's all that matters.
A cold wind blew past as Vedium, his voice fainter than before, continued.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: Why do you care? My son tried to kill Shirone. You have no reason to pity me. So why did you try to give me a chance?

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: …A long time ago, my son died. Right in front of me… his head cut off.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: I don't know. Maybe it was self-defense. Maybe your son was in the wrong, and most people would think so…

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: But emotions aren't like that. No matter how logical you try to be, some things can't be handled rationally.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: I don't know exactly why my body became electricity. But I do know…
Vedium recalled the moment.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: When I learned of Lycan's death, I decided to kill Shirone. That's when it began. Maybe it triggered something… or maybe I'm already dead, and only my memories remain.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Ikael remembered a term Shirone had mentioned.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: Fine-tuning.
Vedium's voice had become so faint that it could barely be heard.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: When you become electricity, you learn many things. Most of them can't be put into words, but you get a sense of it. This world… it seems to hate your son.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Ikael's expression grew serious.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: 'Fine-tuning is applied to those who harbor hatred toward Shirone. Active interference from the outside world…'
The ceiling of this world was halfway open.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Who hired you for the assassination?

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: I can't say. It's a matter of professional pride. But Shirone probably has an idea.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Ikael didn't press further.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: I have nothing more to say. But… when will I finally disappear?

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: Though his body had disintegrated, his thoughts lingered, slowly fading like dissipating smoke.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: Your name is Ikael, right?
She turned to face him.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: This is strange… no, curious. Let me give you some information.
He was standing at the edge of the outer world.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: For gods…
The neon symbols on his mask flickered, forming a face with a tongue sticking out.

blocks.speaker: Vedium
blocks.content: …time flows backward.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: What?
Before Ikael could ask, the neon lights went out, and the cracked mask dissolved into nothingness.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Even an archangel couldn't comprehend his final words.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: She decided to set the thought aside for now, turning to face the inevitable end.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: 'At least… may it have been a life filled with something meaningful.'
Be it good or evil, or something else entirely.
The next morning, Ikael recounted the events of the previous night to Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone had already suspected something.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Giorgi.
The leader of Catacomb, an organization within the armies of hell formed to assassinate Yahweh.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: I didn't tell you earlier because I didn't want you to worry, but there have been other assassination attempts in different regions. However, this time…

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: The fact that they had targeted someone close to him was unsettling.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: 'I've been too careless. No, there's nothing more I could have done. The real issue is…'

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Fine-tuning.
It's impossible to guard against entities with different universal constants.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: That's why Nane went to the outer world…

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: He said, 'For gods, time flows backward.' That was his final message.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No. I can form some hypotheses, but I don't have a definitive answer yet. We'll find out soon, though. I'm heading to the ancient ruins with Zulu.
Canis and Arin had also joined them.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 'For now, Wizard…'
As that thought crossed his mind, he turned just in time to see Wizard walking toward the training grounds.
Her eyes were sunken, and she looked gaunt, as though she hadn't slept all night.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You worked hard.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Teacher.
Wizard approached Shirone directly, while the instructors nervously swallowed their breaths, watching.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: You stayed up all night. Did you make any progress?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: One day…
Wizard's fierce expression suddenly crumpled into one of desperation.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Can I have just one more day?
